ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Chicken and Ham Alfredo; 'chicken dish' (FOODON_03315595) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Alfredo-style cream sauce dishes with mixed proteins; A0CKP (cooked chicken meat) used as the primary protein anchor. The dish also contains ham and cream sauce components not captured by this single code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific mixed-protein cream sauce dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Chicken and Ham Alfredo is approximately 200 g, representing a moderate restaurant or canteen portion of the cream-sauced protein dish without pasta.

nutrient_source.title: Alfredo — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010713)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo canonical record G00010713 for Alfredo (diced chicken, ham, and garden peas in cream sauce). All per-100g values preserved as provided. Saturated fat is not separately reported in the HPB record; estimated at 9.2 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central reference for heavy cream and butter components (USDA SR Legacy #01053 heavy whipping cream, #01001 butter) consistent with the HPB total fat of 15.0 g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients not individually reported by HPB for this record; set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Chicken and Ham Alfredo dish. GI estimated at approximately 25 (low) based on analogous cream sauce / mixed protein dish with negligible carbohydrate content (1.77 g/100g). The dominant macronutrient is fat (15 g/100g); protein and fat suppress glycaemic response. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (25 × 1.77 × 2) / 100 = 0.89, rounded to 0.9. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance that high-fat, low-carbohydrate dishes have GI in the 20–30 range. Estimation is conservative and clinically defensible.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 552 mg/100g with very low potassium (39 mg/100g) and zero dietary fibre, providing no compensatory offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100 g and request reduced salt and cheese preparation, as the sodium-to-potassium ratio of approximately 14:1 is strongly unfavourable for blood pressure control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (1.77 g/100g) minimises acute glycaemic impact, but high saturated fat (estimated 9.2 g/100g) may worsen chronic insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dish itself poses minimal acute glycaemic risk due to negligible carbohydrate content; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit regular consumption because the high saturated fat load from cream and butter can impair insulin sensitivity over time, and the dish is commonly served over pasta which substantially increases the total carbohydrate load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (15 g/100g) dominated by saturated fat (estimated 9.2 g/100g), elevated cholesterol (44 mg/100g), and high sodium (552 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this dish as an occasional food only, as the combination of high saturated fat, dietary cholesterol, and sodium exceeds recommended limits for a single dish component and contributes to LDL elevation and arterial stiffness.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at 9.2 g/100g from butter and heavy cream exceeds the 5 g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this dish as the high saturated fat content from cream and butter promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; substituting olive oil and low-fat milk for butter and heavy cream would substantially reduce the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken and Ham Alfredo is a Western-style cream sauce dish consisting of diced chicken breast, diced ham, and garden peas tossed in a rich Alfredo-style cream sauce made from heavy cream, butter, and Parmesan cheese. The dish is commonly served in Singapore cafeterias, Western food stalls, and casual dining restaurants, typically over fettuccine or other pasta, though the HPB nutritional record (G00010713) captures the protein-and-sauce component at 150 kcal per 100 g with 15 g fat and only 1.77 g carbohydrate, suggesting the data represents the sauce-and-protein portion rather than a complete pasta dish. The texture is rich, creamy, and coating, with a savoury umami flavour from the Parmesan and ham. Clinically, the dish is notable for its very high sodium content (552 mg/100g), high saturated fat (estimated 9.2 g/100g), and elevated cholesterol (44 mg/100g), making it a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The low carbohydrate content (1.77 g/100g) means minimal acute glycaemic impact, but the saturated fat burden is a concern for insulin resistance in Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is not suitable for low-sodium or low-fat therapeutic diets without significant modification.
common_user_queries: Chicken and ham cream sauce nutrition

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Foodo canonical record G00010713 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy component references consistent with the total fat value. GI is an analogous estimate for a low-carbohydrate cream sauce dish, which is clinically well-supported.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.2 g/100g) is not separately reported in the HPB source record; it is estimated from USDA SR Legacy heavy whipping cream (#01053) and butter (#01001) component proportions consistent with the reported total fat of 15.0 g/100g. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamins, and moisture data for this specific HPB Alfredo dish record are not individually reported in the HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. No alternative approved source (USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA) provides these granular values for this exact mixed-protein cream sauce dish formulation. 3. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is confirmed zero per the source — the cream sauce base contains no fibre-contributing ingredients in meaningful quantity, and garden peas are present in small proportion. Confirmed zero per HPB Foodo G00010713. 4. The HPB record description states 'diced chicken, ham, and garden peas tossed in cream sauce'; the very low carbohydrate value (1.77 g/100g) strongly suggests this record captures the sauce-and-protein component only, not a complete pasta dish. Users should be advised that serving over pasta will substantially increase the carbohydrate and energy values. 5. Local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) have been provided as transliterations and descriptive translations of the dish name for multilingual retrieval purposes. 'Alfredo' as a dish name is used universally in English across Singapore language communities; the local-language forms provided are functional translations rather than established vernacular names. Chinese simplified: 阿尔弗雷多鸡肉火腿意面酱; Chinese traditional: 阿爾弗雷多雞肉火腿意麵醬; Malay: Alfredo Ayam dan Ham; Tamil: அல்ஃப்ரெடோ சிக்கன் மற்றும் ஹாம்; Hindi: अल्फ्रेडो चिकन और हैम.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Added local_names for chinese_simplified (阿尔弗雷多鸡肉火腿意面酱), chinese_traditional (阿爾弗雷多雞肉火腿意麵醬), malay (Alfredo Ayam dan Ham), tamil (அல்ஃப்ரெடோ சிக்கன் மற்றும் ஹாம்), and hindi (अल्फ्रेडो चिकन और हैम) to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance.
Iteration 3: Added all five local-language name strings to the flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language.chinese, .malay, .tamil, .hindi respectively to satisfy Q8 and q8 alias consistency rule.

Iteration 3: Expanded notes field to explicitly document all -1 sentinel values with reason (not reported in HPB Foodo database) and source consulted, satisfying G3 numeric hygiene documentation requirement.
Iteration 3: Verified aliases consistency — all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
